question_frequency.rationale: The Pair posed three distinct didactic choices across three turns (approach selection, complexity analysis, alternative approaches), engaging the user at each meaningful decision point.

question_quality.rationale: The complexity question targeting the per-op vs total-space confusion is a genuinely good gotcha. The approach choice and the sort+two-pointers comparison are also substantive, though the framing of B in the third question telegraphed the answer.

scaffolding.rationale: Progression from approach → complexity → alternative is logical, but option B in turn 3 essentially included the full justification ("sorting destroys indices"), spoiling the answer. The complexity question's option B was also a fairly obvious trap.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Conversation flowed naturally with affirming, specific feedback ("O(1) per operation vs O(1) total space — that conflation trips people up"). Tone was warm and the back-and-forth felt collaborative.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code is identical to baseline — correct canonical hash-map solution with passing tests on all three cases.

friction.rationale: About 2x the baseline turns/time (3 turns, 63s vs 2 turns, 29s), but the extra length delivered proportional teaching value via the complexity gotcha and sort+two-pointers contrast.
